mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of July 2005 indicates accommodative monetary stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) above trend signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which increases corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152,186 thousand as of July 2005 reflects sustained employment growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer demand and construction activity, indicating strong aggregate demand that supports equity valuations. The interaction between these two demand components amplifies the signal strength relative to either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 529,766 million and HOUST at 1,608 thousand as of June-July 2005 show concurrent strength
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and residential sectors
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST standardized) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint positive signals fail to predict positive returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 3-month moving average signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization and potential stagflationary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth and inflation risk.
regime_hint: supply-constrained environment with rising input costs
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5d returns in fewer than 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% as of July 2005 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: Instances with UNRATE > 4.0% produce negative 20d returns in fewer than 52% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real cash flow growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 258.82 and FEDFUNDS at 1.55% as of July 2005
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction term (CPIAUCSL × FEDFUNDS standardized) produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured on a quarterly basis above 2.5% annualized rate signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,219.77 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2005
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion above trend growth
failure_condition: Triggered instances with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% produce positive 60d returns in fewer than 58% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,500 thousand units signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, which reduces construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,608 thousand as of June 2005 near cyclical peak
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 20d returns in fewer than 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 18-month window.
